title: T0/T1 Gateway Failover Events
spl:
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:lr_events" earliest=-7d
| search failover OR "HA switch" OR "role change"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, count by lr_name, prev_role, new_role, edge_node
| sort -last_seen
description: Captures active/standby transitions for Tier-0/Tier-1 logical routers for incident correlation and HA validation.
value: Captures active/standby transitions for Tier-0/Tier-1 logical routers for incident correlation and HA validation.
implementation: Normalize syslog/API messages for HA events. Alert on any unplanned failover. Pager for T0.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `vmware_nsx_addon`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `vmware:nsx:lr_events`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize syslog/API messages for HA events. Alert on any unplanned failover. Pager for T0.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:lr_events" earliest=-7d
| search failover OR "HA switch" OR "role change"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, count by lr_name, prev_role, new_role, edge_node
| sort -last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**T0/T1 Gateway Failover Events** — Captures active/standby transitions for Tier-0/Tier-1 logical routers for incident correlation and HA validation.

Documented **Data sources**: `vmware:nsx:lr_events`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `vmware_nsx_addon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:nsx:lr_events. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:nsx:lr_events", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by lr_name, prev_role, new_role, edge_node** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with NSX Manager edge cluster views and the same transport or LR scope as the search.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (failovers), Table (events), Single value (failovers / month).
grandmaExplanation: We record active/standby transitions for Tier-0/Tier-1 logical routers for incident correlation and HA validation — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
